channel.title: # 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka This Week
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 98 English News Articles published between 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-14.

item.title: Staff-level agreement cleared the combined fifth and sixth reviews of the four-year Extended Fund Facility.
item.description: The accord unlocked about $700 million, lifting disbursements to roughly $2.4 billion subject to Executive Board approval. Foreign reserves had rebuilt to $7.3 billion by end-February 2026 after near depletion in 2022, while consumer price growth eased from a peak near 70% in that crisis year to low single digits, strengthening buffers ahead of global shocks.

item.title: Equity indices recorded both a 4.21% jump and a 73-point slide within three sessions at the Colombo Stock Exchange.
item.description: Turnover ranged from Rs 6.61 billion on the up day to Rs 2.96 billion on the softer close, illustrating depth that was twice as large at the weekly high than at the dip. The blue-chip gauge swung by 257.68 points at its best print in the week, while the spot currency hovered around Rs 315 per US dollar, little changed from the prior session, and bond quotes shifted within 9.9%–11.25% across key tenors.

item.title: Lawmakers confronted an estimated Rs. 13.2 billion loss at National Development Bank and supervisors imposed restrictions.
item.description: The lender’s cash dividend was frozen, discretionary outlays curbed, branch expansion halted and an independent forensic audit mandated, measures that followed a rapid escalation from an initial sub–Rs 1 billion shortfall reported earlier in April. The exposure equated to about 0.7% of unaudited total assets, a small balance-sheet share yet large enough to test confidence in internal controls and sector oversight.

item.title: A Select Committee examined legal pathways to restart long-delayed Provincial Council polls.
item.description: The panel heard that incomplete delimitation for a mixed system enacted in 2017 had left no operative provisions to conduct a vote, prompting a request for a compendium of court rulings and legal options from the Attorney General’s Department. Alternatives tabled included repealing the 2017 changes to revive the previous framework, enhancing quotas for women and youth in any revision, and summoning the Delimitation Commission to expedite clarity on boundaries derailed for nearly a decade.

item.title: A Rs. 2.16 billion overhaul of the busiest rail terminus was launched with completion targeted in 15 months.
item.description: Financed by a blend of multilateral and Treasury funding, the upgrade introduced automated ticketing, lifts and overhead bridges, with sanitation overhauls and new buildings aimed at safer flows for large commuter volumes. Once finished, the hub was set to integrate accessibility and digital systems that match international practice, a material step up from legacy facilities still serving hundreds of thousands each day.

item.title: Northern Line services restarted after cyclone damage was fixed under a $5 million grant from India.
item.description: The works formed part of a wider $450 million package and were completed ahead of the Sinhala and Tamil New Year, restoring a vital trunk corridor for passengers and freight across storm-affected districts. By reopening before the festive travel peak, the link reconnected communities at a fraction of the cost of new-build rail, while reinforcing cross-border grant assistance that has spanned transport, fuel and humanitarian support in recent years.

item.title: Solar deployment gathered pace through a new country strategy and fresh capacity additions in rooftops and small hydro.
item.description: A high-level mission mapped a pipeline exceeding 4 GW for 2025–2030, while an 800 kW rooftop at a global financial data campus was commissioned to generate roughly 1,250 MWh a year, trimming emissions by about 850 tonnes—comparable to taking nearly 185 petrol cars off the road or planting over 14,000 trees. In parallel, a 4.5 MW mini-hydro acquisition expanded a listed developer’s portfolio to 26 projects, signalling broader momentum toward non-fossil generation as fuel-import volatility lifted energy security to the fore.

item.title: A private bus operator began fully digital fare collection with card taps and QR scans processed by a leading acquirer.
item.description: Conductors’ handhelds captured contactless payments and mobile wallets with instant confirmation and automated settlement, reducing cash handling risks and leakage in high‑volume, low‑value transactions. Initial routes went live with plans to scale across the fleet, aligning with the national shift to electronic ticketing in public transport where daily rider flows number in the millions.

item.title: A Special Grade SLAS officer was named Commissioner General of Registration of Persons to drive digital ID issuance.
item.description: The appointment replaced an acting arrangement within the ministry and was intended to accelerate rollout of a national credential that underpins e‑government and formal finance access. Moving from a district secretaryship, the official took charge of a department whose throughput will influence service delivery times across banking, telecoms and welfare targeting over the next planning cycle.

item.title: Industry and civil society channelled money and standards into recovery and export readiness.
item.description: The Free Trade Zone Manufacturers Association contributed Rs 5 million to the ‘Rebuilding’ fund for victims of Cyclone Ditwah, adding private capital to relief alongside budget reallocations. Separately, the local arm of a global certifier issued the first Deforestation‑Free Standard group certificate anywhere, after awarding the inaugural single‑entity badge earlier, positioning agri‑industrial supply chains to retain access to the EU under tighter rules while peers across the region still navigate compliance.
